printers.name: X1 Carbon
notes: LiDAR enables automatic flow calibration and first-layer inspection
Open front door + remove top glass when printing PLA with bed >45°C

printers.name: P2S
notes: P1S successor — updated internals, same price point
Compatible with AMS 2 Pro and AMS HT; no AMS Lite support documented
Open front door + remove top glass when printing PLA with bed >45°C

printers.name: P1S
notes: No active chamber temperature control — passive heating only (bed heats chamber)
Open front door + remove top glass when printing PLA with bed >45°C
Hardened steel nozzle + extruder upgrade needed for CF/GF filaments

printers.name: P1P
notes: Open frame — not suitable for ABS, ASA, PA, PC without enclosure modification

printers.name: A1
notes: Bed-slinger — lower max acceleration limits; higher speeds cause ringing on tall prints

printers.name: X1E
notes: Active chamber heating up to 60°C — optimal for PA, PC, ABS, ASA
Industrial-grade variant of X1C — designed for engineering filaments
Open front door + remove top glass when printing PLA with bed >45°C

printers.name: H2C
notes: First-generation AMS works for multi-color printing but does not support AMS drying

printers.name: H2D
notes: First-generation AMS works for multi-color printing but does not support AMS drying

printers.name: H2S
notes: Active chamber heating up to 65°C with HEPA + activated carbon filtration
First-generation AMS works for multi-color printing but does not support AMS drying

printers.name: X2D
notes: Dual hotend printer: main hotend is direct drive; auxiliary hotend uses remote extrusion via PTFE tube
Active chamber temperature control up to 65°C
First-generation AMS works for multi-color printing but does not support AMS drying

printers.name: H2D Pro
notes: Dual hotend printer with tungsten carbide nozzle
Active chamber heating up to 65°C with enhanced filtration and network controls
First-generation AMS works for multi-color printing but does not support AMS drying

printers.name: Ender-3 V3
notes: Open frame — not suitable for ABS, ASA, PA, PC without enclosure modification

printers.name: Ender-3 V4 Combo
notes: Open-frame Ender Series bed-slinger — 220×220×235mm build volume
CFS multicolor support up to 16 colors; combo includes the CFS workflow
Official spec lists 0.4mm nozzle and PEI plate; other quick-swap nozzle sizes are referenced but not enumerated
Built-in camera supported by Creality Wiki camera maintenance/replacement guides; no LiDAR is listed in official product specs or support docs

printers.name: i7
notes: CFS Lite supports up to 4-color printing; CFS Mini is automatic refill only
ABS, ASA, PC, PA and other high-temperature filaments are not recommended on the open frame

printers.name: Kobra X
notes: ACE GEN2 native 4-color, expandable to 19 colors with ACE 2 Pro

printers.name: Kobra 3 V2
notes: ACE V2 with active filament drying

printers.name: Mega X
notes: Legacy printer — stock firmware max acceleration 400 mm/s²; max speed 100 mm/s (60 recommended)

printers.name: Centauri Carbon
notes: Fully enclosed CoreXY with brass-hardened steel nozzle (320°C)
CANVAS multi-color system on Elegoo's roadmap — not shipping yet

printers.name: Centauri Carbon 2
notes: 350°C all-metal direct drive, 20,000 mm/s² CoreXY, passive enclosed Smart Grille — see https://us.elegoo.com/products/centauri-carbon-2
256×256×256mm build volume; 1000W AC heated bed; no active chamber heating
CANVAS 4-color multi-material system is sold separately for the base printer and included with the Combo variant

printers.name: Guider 3 Ultra
notes: IDEX dual extrusion — soluble support capable

printers.name: Aries
notes: Open-frame Ender-5-style Cartesian cube, mapped to the conservative bedslinger tuning bucket
Official Voxelab manual: FDM, 200x200x200mm, 0.4mm standard nozzle, 1.75mm PLA/ABS/PETG, 250C nozzle max, 110C bed max, <=180mm/s
Glass build plate; single 0.4mm nozzle (other sizes not listed in the manual)
max_acceleration 5000 is a conservative app-side cap (no manufacturer acceleration figure is published by Voxelab) - not a claimed manufacturer maximum

printers.name: 2.0 A350
notes: Request was for "Snapmaker 2" (no sub-model). Added as the A350 — the flagship/largest Snapmaker 2.0 — as the conservative representative: it has the LOWEST max bed temp of the 2.0 line (A350 80C < A250 100C < A150 ~100C), so its profile is the safe default. A150/A250 owners get the same nozzle/motion limits and a safe (lower) bed cap.
Snapmaker 2.0: lead-screw-driven 3-in-1 modular Cartesian (FDM + laser + CNC); mapped to the conservative bedslinger motion bucket. Open-frame by default (enclosure is a separate accessory).
Official spec: FDM, 0.4mm direct-drive hot end, 275C max nozzle, 80C max bed (A350 hardware limit), single-extrusion build volume ~320x335x310mm.

max_speed 120 and max_acceleration 3000 are from Snapmaker's official firmware motion config (Snapmaker/Snapmaker2-Controller Marlin Configuration.h: DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE X/Y=120 mm/s, DEFAULT_MAX_ACCELERATION X/Y=3000 mm/s2). Real-world print speed is ~60mm/s due to lead-screw kinematics.

printers.name: Creator 5 Pro
notes: Enclosed CoreXY with 4 independent direct-drive toolheads (FlashSwap tool-changing) — mapped to the multi_color_systems 'toolchanger' tier.
Active heated chamber up to 65C; HEPA13 + activated-carbon filtration; built-in 1280x720 camera.
Official spec: FDM, 256x256x256mm, 320C max nozzle, 120C max bed, 65C max chamber, PEI textured flexible steel plate, nozzles 0.25/0.4/0.6/0.8mm.

max_speed 300 mm/s is FlashForge's published spec-table value (flashforge.com + flashforge.com.hk). Marketing/reviews cite '600 mm/s' as a travel/peak figure, not steady print speed; the conservative spec-table 300 is used.
